title: Loving Like Jesus
summary: “And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself. And he said unto him, Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live. But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?” (Luke 10:27-29). “A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.” (John 13:34). “This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.” (John 13:34) There are two categories of people Jesus commanded us to love unconditionally after God: our neighbors and the people of God. Sometimes the commandments that appear to be the easiest to understand turn out to be the hardest to implement. Let us go back to the basics of God: “And we have known and believed the love that God hath to us. God is love; and he that dwelleth in love dwelleth in God, and God in him” (I John 4:16). The question is: have we known and believed the love that God hath to us? In other words, we cannot love others if we have not experienced the love of the Lord Jesus Christ. What was the overpowering force that drew the apostles to leave everything and follow Jesus? What is the power that still attracts millions worldwide to follow Jesus? The answer is his love.
